parameters.query:
SELECT 
  (
    SELECT id 
    FROM public.catalogue_products 
    WHERE name = '{{ $json.name.replace(/'/g, "''") }}'
    LIMIT 1
  ) AS id,
  '{{ $json.name.replace(/'/g, "''") }}' AS name,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 
    FROM public.catalogue_products 
    WHERE name = '{{ $json.name.replace(/'/g, "''") }}'
  ) AS has_exist;

nodes.name: CHECK IF THE GAME NAME EXIST

parameters.query:
SELECT
    DISTINCT ON (pu.id)
    cpmd.id AS meta_data_id,
    cpmd.product_id,
    cpmd.meta_data_id,
    cmd.id AS catalogue_meta_data_id,
    cmd.publisher_organisation_id,
    po.id AS organisation_id,
    po.name AS organisation_name,
    po.active AS organisation_active,
    pm.id AS membership_id,
    pm.user_id,
    pm.role,
    pu.id AS publisher_user_id,
    pu.email,
    pu.first_name,
    pu.last_name
FROM catalogue_product_meta_data cpmd
JOIN catalogue_meta_data cmd
    ON cpmd.meta_data_id = cmd.id
JOIN publisher_organisations po
    ON cmd.publisher_organisation_id = po.id
    AND po.active = true
JOIN publisher_memberships pm
    ON po.id = pm.organisation_id
    AND pm.user_id NOT IN (15001, 82)  -- exlude proxy.admin@keymailer.co
JOIN publisher_users pu
    ON pm.user_id = pu.id
WHERE cpmd.product_id = {{ $json.id }};

nodes.name: TABLE RELATIONSHIPS
nodes.notes:
GET THE PUBLISHER ORGANIZATION AND ITS USER USING CATALOGUE_PRODUCTS ID.
EXCLUDE THE 15001(KEYMAILER EMAIL)
EXCLUDE THE 82 (KEYMAILER EMAIL)
MAKE SURE THE ORGANIZATION IS ACTIVE (TRUE)

TABLES:
catalogue_product_meta_data
catalogue_meta_data
publisher_organisations
publisher_memberships
publisher_users

parameters.query:
WITH 
search_term AS (
    SELECT CASE 
        WHEN '{{$json.publisher}}' IN ('', 'null', 'NULL') 
        THEN '{{$json.developer}}'
        ELSE '{{$json.publisher}}'
    END AS term
),
publisher_match AS (
    SELECT 
        po.id,
        po.name
    FROM public.publisher_organisations po, search_term
    WHERE po.name ILIKE '%' || search_term.term || '%'
      AND po.active = true
),
results AS (
    SELECT 
        {{$json.steam_appid}} AS steam_appid,
        po.name AS organisations_name,
        pm.role,
        pu.id AS user_id,
        pu.email,
        pu.first_name,
        pu.last_name,
        true AS "publisherHasFound"
    FROM publisher_match po
    JOIN public.publisher_memberships pm ON pm.organisation_id = po.id
    JOIN public.publisher_users pu ON pu.id = pm.user_id
    WHERE pm.user_id NOT IN (15001, 82)
)
SELECT * FROM results
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    {{$json.steam_appid}} AS steam_appid,
    (SELECT term FROM search_term) AS organisations_name,
    NULL AS role,
    NULL AS user_id,
    NULL AS email,
    NULL AS first_name,
    NULL AS last_name,
    false AS "publisherHasFound"
WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM results);

parameters.content:
## Lead Generation - Finding Games

**Purpose:**
- The purpose of this workflow is to get the game name from competitors, check if it exists in Madmin, and if it does, retrieve the contact. If it does not exist, the workflow checks Steam, retrieves the publisher from Steam, and then gets the contact in Madmin. If the game name still does not exist, it is added to the unmatched games list.

## Documentation
This will run everyday, :
- 10:30 AM UK time OR 5:30 PM PHT

- Retrieve data from the scraped campaign sheet where competitor data is stored, and only get today’s data.

- Check if the game exists in Madmin. If yes, retrieve the contact.

- If not, check Steam, get the publisher, and use that publisher to retrieve the contact in Madmin.

- If the game does not exist in both, store it in the unmatched games list.

## Lead Generation - Finding Contacts

**Purpose:**
- The purpose of this workflow is to get the game’s publisher from Madmin and Steam, and use the publisher to check if it exists in both Intercom and ActiveCampaign.

## Documentation
This will run everyday, :
- 11:00 AM UK time OR 6:00 PM PHT

- Retrieve data from the Madmin and Steam sheet

- Check if the publisher exists in Intercom; if yes, retrieve the contacts.

- Check if the publisher exists in ActiveCampaign; if yes, retrieve the contacts.

- If the publisher does not exist in either platform, store it in the unmatched contacts list, then use it for manual enrichment in Clay.

- Send a Slack notification, if there's new data to enrich.

nodes.name: STEAM MATCHING APP ID V2
nodes.notes: check if steam_appid is match with id, if yes retrieve the game_name_from_sheet

nodes.name: STEAM MATCHING GAME NAME
nodes.notes: check if game_name_from_sheet field and name field is match, if yes, retrieve the url and source

nodes.name: INTERCOM: MATCHING ORG NAME
nodes.notes: check if there's a matching org name, if yes retrieve the game name, source, url

nodes.name: NO FOUND: MATCHING ORG
nodes.notes: retrieve the game name, source and url if there's a matching org name

parameters.content:
## Lead Generation - Slack Notification

**Purpose:**
- The purpose of this workflow is to retrieve today’s data from Intercom and ActiveCampaign, then send a Slack notification for review.

It also retrieves data from Unmatched Contacts; if there is a pending status, it sends a Slack notification.
